About Our Service

Directed by Mr. Biju

Providing high-yield groundwater using traditional manual hand-boring operations.

At **FOS Hand Borewell Services**, based out of Kirathoor Eathampalangi, we believe in 'Final Operation Success'. We specialize in manual hand borewells that allow us to dig in narrow sites, backyard gardens, agricultural farms, and inside house structures where heavy vehicle rigs cannot step. Mr. Biju and his experienced local team work carefully to find premium water seams and install long-lasting casing pipes.

No Heavy Truck Damage
Indoor & Narrow Site Fit
Affordable Rural Rates
Complete Casing Setup
What We Do

Hand Boring & Rural Water Services

We offer customized groundwater sourcing using traditional, non-destructive hand drilling methods suitable for rural and domestic sites.

Hand Borewell Drilling

Manual lever-based boring down to water seams (typically 80-250 feet). Ideal for backyards, basements, agricultural land, and small residential spaces.

Call Mr. Biju

Borewell Cleaning

Flushing out mud, silt, and blockages from existing hand borewells using high-pressure manual pumps or air compressors to restore fresh water flow.

WhatsApp Us

Pump Installation

Fitting high-efficiency single-phase submersible pumps, jet motors, or hand-operated suction pumps tailored for rural homes.

Call Mr. Biju

Water Finding Survey

Traditional water divining coupled with local geological analysis of Eathampalangi soils to identify the highest yielding spot to drill.

WhatsApp Us

Casing & Filter Fitting

Inserting premium quality PVC casing pipes and slotted sand filters to prevent clay collapse and keep your drinking water crystal clear.

Call Mr. Biju

FAQ Panel

Boring Queries Answered

Common questions regarding hand boring process, casing pipes, and pricing in the Eathampalangi region.

How deep can a hand borewell be drilled?
With manual hand boring methods, we can typically drill depths between 80 feet to 250 feet depending on the soil layers. In Eathampalangi and surrounding areas, stable groundwater is usually successfully reached within this range.
What are the advantages of hand boring over rig vehicles?
Hand boring uses lightweight, manual setups which do not require road access. We can drill inside building basements, under stairs, or in compact backyards without destroying walls, lawns, or overhead cables. It is also much quieter and very cost-effective.
How long does it take to complete one hand borewell?
A standard hand boring operation takes about 2 to 4 days to complete, including site survey, manual drilling, casing pipe fitting, sand filter placement, and testing the water yield.
What does "FOS" mean?
FOS stands for **Final Operation Success**. It is our core commitment to Mr. Biju's clients: we do not leave the site until we successfully source fresh water and ensure the system operates perfectly.
